comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Morning Report, 6:51 AM

Good morning Sugarloafers,

Spring has sprung, and our mountain is going to be showing off today.

Temperatures will reach a high of 45 at the base and 37 at the summit under mostly sunny skies. Something about temps this warm and sun this nice makes you want to sit on the deck with a cocktail. Thank goodness for Bullwinkle's.

The wind starts the day mild but will increase as the day goes on. All lifts are set to rock and roll right now, but we'll keep an eye on them. Scroll down for the full schedule.

Groomers had their noses to the grindstone last night getting the good stuff. A recent favorite has been Skidder, Haulback. and Gondi. All those got a sweep or two of the tiller last night.

We should start the day off with some cord, but it's going to get skied off quick. As temperatures rise, the snow will become increasingly soft and spring-like. Today's surface will be quite fun, and I believe some corn snow will be in our future. Yummy.

Narrow Gauge will be closed at Pick Pole for racing.

Uphill travel is allowed to Spillway X-Cut via the Superquad Route and the West Mountain Route.

While we still have a whole bunch of skiing and riding left this year, lock in the best prices for next season's New England Pass. The sale ends in 3 days, so snag the deal while you can. Grab them through our quick links.

Stay stoked,
Shannon

comment.name: Uphill
comment.text:
Uphill travel is open to Spillway X-Cut via the West Mountain Route and the SuperQuad Route.

Visit the Uphill Policy page for detailed directions.

Note: All day-ticket uphill travelers must pick up their 1-day wristband prior to starting their ascent with Guest Services located in the base lodge. Your wristband must be visible at all times. For more information, visit the Uphill Policy page.

trail.name: Lower Sheer Boom
trail.comment: Please note Lower Sheer Boom refers to the section between Tote X-Cut and Peavy X-Cut

trail.name: Upper Narrow Gauge
trail.comment: Closed at Pick Pole for Racing

park.name: Drop Line Park
park.comment: "Fun Zone" featuring low-angle bumps, spines, rollers, and banked turns
